Records.description: Two-loop planar double-box with six internal propagators (five massive of equal mass $m$, one massless) and four external legs (three massless $p_1,p_2,p_3$ and one massive $p_4$ with $p_4^2 = m_h^2$). Originally computed in Bonciani et al. (1605.03157). The IBP reduction yields four master integrals; in $d=4$ the equation for $I_4$ decouples and the maximal cut of $I_3$ (which contains a dotted internal triangle subdiagram) vanishes, reducing the system to two coupled first-order homogeneous equations for $I_1$ and $I_2$. The maximal cut of $I_1$ is computed by first cutting the inner one-loop triangle and then localizing the remaining three delta-functions, producing a one-fold integral over a square root of a quartic in $k_0$. After standard change of variables one obtains $F_1 = \operatorname{EllipticK}(\omega)/\sqrt{s^2(m_h^2-t)^2 - 4 m^2 s [m_h^2(s-t)+t(s+t) - 2 \sqrt{-s t u m_h^2}]}$ (Eq. 4.36), with $\omega$ given by Eq. 4.37. A second independent solution $F_2 = \operatorname{EllipticK}(1-\omega)/\sqrt{\ldots}$ is also constructed, and equivalent representations $\tilde{F}_1, \tilde{F}_2$ involving $\operatorname{EllipticK}(1/\omega), \operatorname{EllipticK}(1-1/\omega)$ are given (Eq. 4.43). The result agrees with Bonciani et al. for parameter $\alpha=1$.
Records.familyName: elliptic planar two-loop double box with massive internal propagators

Names: elliptic planar two-loop double box with massive internal propagators
